binaries.title: ksecpkg.sys - Missing minimum-length validation before TLS/DTLS record header reads (CWE-125) fixed

changed_functions.name: Tls13DecryptHandler
key_changes: Adds an explicit minimum-record-length check before dereferencing fixed header byte offsets of the attacker-supplied record
New 'cmp edx,5; jb' at 0x140001723 (TLS header floor) before reading rec[1]/rec[3]/rec[4]
New 'cmp edx,0Dh; jb' at 0x140001865 (DTLS header floor, 13 bytes) before reading rec[0xB]/rec[0xC]
Unpatched had only 'cmp r8d,edx' at 0x1C000AE5E (cbBuffer >= cipher_overhead ctx[0x40]+ctx[0x44]) before those reads
edx/r8d is the record buffer length (SPBuffer cbBuffer at +4)
changed_functions.security_notes: The unpatched build reads fixed record-header offsets (up to +0xC for DTLS) guarded only by a cipher-overhead comparison that does not guarantee the record is long enough; a truncated record can reach the reads and cause an out-of-bounds read of the input buffer. The patched build adds explicit >=5 (TLS) and >=13 (DTLS) minimum-length floors before the reads, returning STATUS_INVALID_TOKEN (0x80090318). CWE-125 out-of-bounds read; patched build is stricter.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; --- Unpatched (0x1C000AE5E) ---
cmp     r8d, edx            ; cbBuffer >= cipher_overhead (ONLY guard)
jnb     0x1C000AE70
movzx   ecx, byte ptr [rdi+0Bh]  ; DTLS read, no >=13 guard (0x1C000AE8E)
movzx   eax, byte ptr [rdi+0Ch]
; --- Patched (0x140001723 / 0x140001865) ---
cmp     edx, 5              ; NEW TLS header floor
jb      0x1400018F3         ; -> 0x80090318
...
cmp     edx, 0Dh            ; NEW DTLS header floor (13)
jb      0x140001908         ; -> 0x80090318
movzx   ecx, byte ptr [r14+0Bh]  ; now guarded (0x14000186E)
movzx   eax, byte ptr [r14+0Ch]

changed_functions.name: TlsDecryptHandler
key_changes: Same minimum-record-length fix: new 'cmp edx,5' at 0x1400019FD and 'cmp edx,0Dh' at 0x140001A3A
Unpatched reads header offsets rec[0xB]/rec[0xC] (e.g. at 0x1C00051D9/0x1C00051DE) and rec[3]/rec[4] with no >=5/>=13 guard
changed_functions.security_notes: Same CWE-125 out-of-bounds read hardening as Tls13DecryptHandler, applied to the TLS 1.0-1.2 record decrypt handler.

changed_functions.name: SslUnsealMessageStream
key_changes: Same minimum-record-length fix at two record-parse sites: 'cmp r8d,5' at 0x140001234 and 0x14000154E; 'cmp r8d,0Dh' at 0x140001385 and 0x140001587
Unpatched reads header offsets rec[0xB]/rec[0xC] at 0x1C00014F0/0x1C00014F4 (and others) with no >=5/>=13 guard
changed_functions.security_notes: Same CWE-125 out-of-bounds read hardening for the stream (TLS) unseal path.

changed_functions.name: SslUnsealMessageConnection
key_changes: Same minimum-record-length fix: 'cmp ecx,5' at 0x140004F8F and 'cmp ecx,0Dh' at 0x140004FDC
Unpatched reads rec[0xB]/rec[0xC] at 0x1C000A8FC/0x1C000A900 with no >=5/>=13 guard
changed_functions.security_notes: Same CWE-125 out-of-bounds read hardening for the connection unseal path.

changed_functions.name: DigestKernelHTTPHelper
key_changes: The loop over the fixed 24-entry internal table at [arg1+0xA2] is unchanged in behavior; the loop counter edx runs 0..0x17 with a compile-time-constant bound (cmp edx,0x18), it is NOT an attacker-supplied element type

Both builds skip the identical index set {7,8,10} and copy all other indices when the entry's leading word is non-zero; unpatched via ((edx-7)&~3)==0 && edx!=9, patched via edx==8 || edx==10 || edx==7
DigestKernelGetNextSeqNumber (@0x1C0006E90) was inlined; the inlined code performs the same fast-mutex-protected increment of [ctx+0x54]
Register allocation differs (rsi/rdi roles swapped)
changed_functions.security_notes: No behavioral change. The originally reported 'attacker-controlled element type dispatch' does not exist: edx is a fixed loop index (0..23) over an internal table, and the copy-skip predicate covers the identical value set {7,8,10} in both builds. The other differences are inlining of a helper that already used the same locking and register reallocation.

changed_functions.name: strcat_s
key_changes: The copy loop is byte-for-byte identical in behavior in both builds; it writes one byte, returns success on the source NUL, otherwise decrements SizeInBytes and returns error 34 (ERANGE) at exactly zero
The patched build only removed dead instructions: 'mov rax,rdx; test rax,rax' in the destination-scan loop and 'test rdx,rdx; jnz' in the copy loop (rdx is provably 0 on the fall-through)
Every caller passes a compile-time constant size (0x3001 or 0x1001); the bound is not attacker-controlled
changed_functions.security_notes: No off-by-one in either build. The copy loop performs exactly SizeInBytes - strlen(dst) in-bounds writes and then returns ERANGE at the boundary. The originally reported 'else if (!arg2) break' one-iteration-late break does not exist; it is a misreading of the dead 'test rdx,rdx; jnz' pair that the patched build removed. The unpatched and patched decompilations are logically identical.

changed_functions.name: DigestCreateChalResp
key_changes: Digest response building reorganized
Uses fixed compile-time-constant buffers (0x3001 / 0x1001) with sprintf_s and strcat_s; buffer sizes unchanged
changed_functions.security_notes: No security boundary change. Buffer sizes are constants and the bounded string helpers it calls are unchanged in behavior.

changed_functions.name: KerbMakeSignatureToken
key_changes: Kerberos signature-token formatting reorganized as part of the crypto refactor
Patched build adds the KerberosCryptoPolicy class family and Old-suffixed signature variants
Conditional logic restructured; no bounds or validation added or removed
changed_functions.security_notes: Part of the servicing/compiler rebuild that introduces the KerberosCryptoPolicy C++ class hierarchy. No attacker-reachable security boundary change identified.

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureReporting_IncrementOpportunityInCache
key_changes: WIL feature-usage telemetry; register allocation changes only
No functional change

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureReporting_IncrementUsageInCache
key_changes: WIL feature-usage telemetry; register allocation changes only
No functional change

changed_functions.name: NtLmInitKernelContext
key_changes: Register renaming (rdx <-> rcx swaps)
Allocation sizes bounded (< 0xffff) in both versions

added: TlsDecryptMessage (extracted record-decrypt helper present only in the patched build; carries the same >=5 / >=0xD length floors)

security_findings.entry_point: Schannel TLS/DTLS record decryption on an established context (SslUnsealMessage* / Tls*DecryptHandler)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Missing length validation before fixed-offset dereference of attacker-controlled input (out-of-bounds read)
security_findings.description: The Schannel record-decryption handlers read fixed byte offsets of an inbound record to obtain the version/type and declared length: offsets +1/+3/+4 for a TLS record (needs >=5 bytes) and +0xB/+0xC for a DTLS record (needs >=13 bytes). In the unpatched build the only length check before these reads is cbBuffer >= cipher_overhead (ctx[0x40]+ctx[0x44]), which does not guarantee the record contains the dereferenced header bytes. A truncated record that satisfies the overhead check but is shorter than the header reaches the reads and causes an out-of-bounds read of the input buffer. The patched build adds explicit 'cmp <recordlen>,5' and 'cmp <recordlen>,0Dh' floors before the reads at every record-parse entry point, returning STATUS_INVALID_TOKEN (0x80090318) for a too-short record. The out-of-bounds bytes feed a declared record length that is subsequently re-validated against cbBuffer, so the operation is a read (no write primitive).
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. An established TLS or DTLS context handled by the kernel Schannel provider. 2. Send an encrypted record whose buffer length (SPBuffer cbBuffer at +4) satisfies cbBuffer >= cipher_overhead but is shorter than the header dereferenced: <5 bytes for TLS, or <13 bytes for a record whose version byte marks it DTLS (rec[1]==0xFE). 3. Reachability of a true out-of-bounds access depends on the negotiated cipher's cipher_overhead being smaller than the header length.
security_findings.primitive: Kernel out-of-bounds read (CWE-125) of a few bytes adjacent to the record buffer; realistic outcome is a read fault (DoS) or a parsing decision influenced by adjacent memory. No write primitive.
security_findings.evidence:
; Unpatched Tls13DecryptHandler (0x1C000ADC4): only guard
cmp     r8d, edx            ; 0x1C000AE5E  cbBuffer >= cipher_overhead
jnb     0x1C000AE70
movzx   ecx, byte ptr [rdi+0Bh]  ; 0x1C000AE8E  DTLS read, no >=13 guard
movzx   eax, byte ptr [rdi+0Ch]  ; 0x1C000AE92
; Patched Tls13DecryptHandler (0x140001680): added floors
cmp     edx, 5              ; 0x140001723  NEW TLS floor -> 0x80090318 if below
cmp     edx, 0Dh            ; 0x140001865  NEW DTLS floor (13) -> 0x80090318 if below
movzx   ecx, byte ptr [r14+0Bh]  ; 0x14000186E  now guarded
movzx   eax, byte ptr [r14+0Ch]  ; 0x140001873
; Same two constants added in: TlsDecryptHandler (0x1400019FD / 0x140001A3A), SslUnsealMessageStream (0x140001234,0x14000154E / 0x140001385,0x140001587), SslUnsealMessageConnection (0x140004F8F / 0x140004FDC). None present in the unpatched counterparts.

breakpoints.why: The only pre-read length check (cbBuffer >= cipher_overhead). Inspect r8d (cbBuffer) and edx (overhead).
Unguarded DTLS header read at rec[0xB]/rec[0xC]; reached when rec[1]==0xFE with only the overhead check upstream.
The new DTLS >=13 floor (cmp edx,0Dh) that gates the same reads in the patched build.

watch: cbBuffer = SPBuffer length field at +4 (record length)
record bytes rec[1] (version), rec[0xB]/rec[0xC] (DTLS length), rec[3]/rec[4] (TLS length)
key_offsets: 0x1C000AE5E (unpatched): cmp r8d,edx - the sole length guard before header reads
0x140001723 (patched): cmp edx,5 - new TLS header floor
0x140001865 (patched): cmp edx,0Dh - new DTLS header floor
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Drive an inbound TLS/DTLS record through a kernel Schannel context and supply a record shorter than the header offsets it dereferences while still satisfying cbBuffer >= cipher_overhead.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Unpatched reaches movzx byte ptr [rec+0xB]/[rec+0xC] (or [rec+3]/[rec+4]) and reads past the buffer end; patched returns STATUS_INVALID_TOKEN (0x80090318) at the new cmp before the read.
debugging_aids.notes: Whether a truncated record can pass the overhead check while being below the header length depends on the negotiated cipher's cipher_overhead value (ctx[0x40]+ctx[0x44]), which was not established here; hence Medium severity.

downgraded_findings.original_claim: Authentication token element-type validation bypass: unpatched range check (lea eax,[rdx-7]; test eax,0xfffffffc) let attacker-controlled element types 0x7 and 0xa reach a data-copy path, which the patch added validation for.
downgraded_findings.why_downgraded: The loop is not driven by attacker input. edx is a fixed loop index initialized to 0 and bounded by the compile-time constant 0x18 (24), iterating an internal table at [arg1+0xA2] at 0x10-byte stride. Both builds skip the identical index set {7,8,10} and copy all other indices under the same non-zero-word condition: unpatched via ((edx-7)&~3)==0 && edx!=9, patched via explicit edx==8||edx==10||edx==7. No index is newly rejected or accepted, no validation was added or removed, and no memory-safety primitive exists. The only additional delta is inlining of DigestKernelGetNextSeqNumber (@0x1C0006E90), whose body performs the same fast-mutex-protected increment of [ctx+0x54] that the patched code now emits inline, plus register reallocation.

downgraded_findings.original_claim: Off-by-one in bounded string concatenation: unpatched 'else if (!arg2) break' fired one iteration late, writing one byte past the buffer.
downgraded_findings.why_downgraded: There is no off-by-one in either build. The copy loop writes a byte, returns success on the source NUL, otherwise decrements the remaining size and returns error 34 (ERANGE) when it reaches 0; it performs exactly SizeInBytes - strlen(dst) in-bounds writes. The unpatched and patched decompilations are logically identical; the only instruction-level difference is removal of dead code ('mov rax,rdx; test rax,rax' and an unreachable 'test rdx,rdx; jnz' where rdx is provably 0). The claimed 'else if (!arg2) break' is a misreading of that dead pair. The size argument is a compile-time constant (0x3001/0x1001) at every call site, so it is not attacker-controlled.

summary: Audit result: the two changes originally flagged in this report are NOT security fixes, and the real delivered fix is elsewhere. In DigestKernelHTTPHelper (@0x1C0006F00 -> 0x14000956C) the flagged loop iterates a fixed 24-entry internal table by a compile-time-bounded index (edx, 0..0x17), not an attacker-supplied element type; both builds skip the identical index set {7,8,10} and merely re-encode the skip predicate. In strcat_s (@0x1C0003480 -> 0x140005768) both builds implement an identical, correct bounded concatenation; the only delta is removal of dead instructions, and every caller supplies a compile-time-constant size. The genuine security-relevant change is in the Schannel TLS/DTLS record-decryption handlers (Tls13DecryptHandler @0x1C000ADC4 -> 0x140001680, TlsDecryptHandler @0x1C0001770 -> 0x140001980, SslUnsealMessageStream @0x1C0001330 -> 0x140001120, SslUnsealMessageConnection @0x1C000A6BC -> 0x140004D50, plus a new extracted helper TlsDecryptMessage): the patched build adds explicit minimum-record-length floors (>=5 for a TLS header, >=13 for a DTLS header) before dereferencing fixed record-header offsets, whereas the unpatched build guarded those reads only with a cipher-overhead comparison that does not guarantee the bytes exist. This is a CWE-125 out-of-bounds read hardening; the patched build is stricter. Final verdict: real fix = missing minimum-length validation before TLS/DTLS record header reads (CWE-125, Medium); the two originally-reported findings are downgraded to No security-relevant change.
